exercise: Active play sessions (fetch, tug) 20 min daily
Off-leash time in a safe area 2-3x per week
Swimming or running for high-energy breeds
nutrition: Fresh water available at all times
grooming: Brush coat 2-3x per week (daily for long-haired breeds)
Bathe every 4-6 weeks or as needed
Clean ears weekly to prevent infection
socialization: Social play with other dogs weekly
Exposure to new environments and people

exercise: Interactive play 2x daily, 10-15 min each session
Wand toys or laser pointer sessions daily
Cat trees or climbing structures for activity
nutrition: Wet food at least 1x daily for hydration
Fresh water or water fountain available always
Avoid onions, garlic, grapes, and xylitol
grooming: Brush coat 2-3x per week (daily for long-haired cats)
Wipe eyes and ears gently as needed
socialization: Lap time and bonding 15+ min daily
Introduce toys and new stimuli regularly

exercise: Out-of-cage time or supervised free roam as appropriate
nutrition: Avoid foods toxic to the specific species
grooming: Coat, feathers, or scales cared for as species requires
Nail or beak trimming as needed
health: Annual vet checkup with exotic/small animal specialist
Parasite prevention as recommended by vet
socialization: Mental stimulation through foraging or puzzle toys
